summary: Replaced raw ftDonkey MotionState table flag and move-id hex constants with named Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags and FtMoveId expressions, centralizing Donkey Kong motion flag combinations in ftDonkey/forward.h. The PR also removed redundant ftLink motion-flag aliases and updated Link/Young Link motion tables to use the underlying equivalent constants directly. No PR body or review discussion was present in the slice.
what_changed: Added many static Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags const definitions to src/melee/ft/chara/ftDonkey/forward.h, including shared bases such as ftDk_MF_Special, ftDk_MF_Cargo, ftDk_MF_CargoThrow, ftDk_MF_CargoWait, ftDk_MF_Move_53, and per-motion variants such as ftDk_MF_SpecialN, ftDk_MF_SpecialAirN, ftDk_MF_CargoThrowF, ftDk_MF_MS_342, and ftDk_MF_MS_348.
Updated src/melee/ft/chara/ftDonkey/ftDk_Init.c to include forward.h and replace raw first-column motion flags like 0x00480000, 0x00A80035, and 0x00340211 with named ftDk_MF_* constants.

Updated ftDk_Init_MotionStateTable move-id fields from raw shifted hex values like 0x35000000, 0x39000000, 0x12000000, and 0x15000000 to expressions such as FtMoveId_Unk53 * (1 << 24), FtMoveId_Unk57 * (1 << 24), FtMoveId_SpecialN * (1 << 24), and FtMoveId_SpecialLw * (1 << 24).
Preserved explicit extra bit behavior in some move-id fields by writing expressions such as (FtMoveId_Unk53 * (1 << 24)) | (1 << 22) instead of leaving the original 0x35400000 literal.
Removed redundant ftLink forward.h aliases whose values were exactly another constant, including ftLk_MF_SpecialSThrowEmpty, ftLk_MF_SpecialAirSCatch, ftLk_MF_SpecialAirHi, and ftLk_MF_Zair.

Updated ftLink and ftCLink motion tables to use non-aliased equivalents directly: ftLk_MF_SpecialSCatch for SpecialSThrowEmpty state 304, ftLk_MF_SpecialAirSThrow for Link state 306, ftLk_MF_SpecialHi for Link state 309, and FtStateChange_FreezeState for Zair state 312.
smart_moves: Factored repeated Donkey Kong motion flag bitmasks into composable named constants, making related groups obvious: specials share ftDk_MF_Special, cargo actions share ftDk_MF_Cargo/ftDk_MF_CargoWait, and cargo throws share ftDk_MF_CargoThrow.
Used existing enum-style move identifiers in table entries rather than opaque high-byte literals, improving searchability and reducing the risk of misreading move categories.
Kept ATTRIBUTE_USED on many static const header definitions, which is important for constants only referenced in motion table initializer contexts or needed for matching/retention behavior.
Removed one-layer ftLink aliases instead of proliferating equivalent names, which reduced header noise while preserving table semantics through direct substitution.
decomp_lessons: For fighter MotionState tables, replace raw flag hex with named Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags combinations once the bit meanings are known; this makes large tables reviewable and cross-character patterns easier to compare.
When a motion table's move-id field is a shifted FtMoveId, prefer explicit FtMoveId_* * (1 << 24) expressions; keep non-move-id bits explicit with OR terms such as | (1 << 22).
Group per-character motion flags in the character forward.h when they are needed by the init table and can be composed from common FtStateChange_* bits.
If a named motion flag is only an exact alias for another constant and provides little semantic value, replacing uses with the underlying constant can simplify future maintenance.

Cross-character cleanup can fall out of one fighter's cleanup: this ftDonkey pass also touched ftLink and ftCLink because their forward.h aliases and motion tables exposed redundant motion flag naming.
naming_conventions: Donkey Kong motion flag constants use prefix ftDk_MF_ followed by the action or motion-state group, e.g. ftDk_MF_SpecialN, ftDk_MF_SpecialAirHi, ftDk_MF_CargoThrowD.
Shared base flag combinations are named as reusable bases, e.g. ftDk_MF_Special, ftDk_MF_Cargo, ftDk_MF_CargoThrow, ftDk_MF_CargoWait, ftDk_MF_MS_342_Base.
Unknown or not-yet-semantically-named states remain tied to state numbers with names like ftDk_MF_MS_341, ftDk_MF_MS_350, ftDk_MF_MS_386 rather than inventing unsupported action names.
Move-id table expressions use existing FtMoveId_* symbols such as FtMoveId_Unk53, FtMoveId_Unk57, FtMoveId_SpecialN, FtMoveId_SpecialS, FtMoveId_SpecialHi, and FtMoveId_SpecialLw.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted raw initializer constants to symbolically equivalent constant expressions without changing the MotionState table structure.
Used const flag composition in headers to make bitmask identity explicit while preserving values expected by matching.
Marked many static const flag definitions with ATTRIBUTE_USED, a tactic that can prevent compiler/linker elimination or warning issues for constants introduced primarily for initializer readability.
Represented high-byte move IDs as FtMoveId_* * (1 << 24), matching the existing packed motion table format while avoiding opaque hex.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other fighter MotionState tables still using raw hex motion flags instead of Fighter_MotionStateChangeFlags constants.
Search for remaining move-id fields encoded as raw 0xNN000000 literals that can be rewritten as FtMoveId_* * (1 << 24).
Audit character forward.h files for redundant one-to-one motion flag aliases similar to the removed ftLk_MF_* aliases.
Investigate unknown Donkey Kong motion labels ftDk_MS_Unk341 through ftDk_MS_Unk348 and ftDk_MS_386 for stronger semantic names if later evidence exists.
